dimensions.note: No secrets stored or vaulted. Per-account credentials come from a named profile via fromIni or the ambient chain (undefined). Read-only surface — a *-readonly profile is sufficient (documented), so it scopes credentials as narrowly as possible. roles/profiles/stackLookupRegions/region/requiredProfileSuffix are non-secret config. Nothing sensitive is logged (account ids, role names, and ARNs are non-secret identifiers); error messages are surfaced but carry no credential material.

warn on every degraded path (profile suffix refusal, credentials failure, per-role read failure), each naming the profile/account/role and the classified message; info on completion with role/present/error counts. Structured placeholders throughout; ambient profile rendered as '<ambient>'; no secret values logged.

Fails closed before any AWS call on the two sanctioned config errors: effectiveRoles throws on empty roles, and audit throws a descriptive error when stackLookupRegions is unset or empty (the wrong region misclassifies CFN-managed as 'manual', so there is no safe default). Both throws precede any facade construction (asserted by tripwire tests). Within the sweep, per-account credential failures and per-role read failures are classified (classifyError) and written as scan_error rows rather than aborting the run — correct failure isolation for a fan-out audit. findManagingStack treats a region miss/error as a non-owning region (best-effort), not a hard failure.

tests/ holds a pure-logic unit suite (evaluateCompliance pass+fail, inferMechanism all four branches, parseTrustPolicy URL-encoded/plain/string-or-array/dedupe/unparseable, classifyError including the access-denied-wins-over-auth-expiry precedence regression with a fixture tripping BOTH predicates, tagsFromAws, accountNameFromProfile, policy/role/error key construction) and a smoke suite driving runAudit through an in-memory IamApi (one row per account x role, present vs missing, credential-failure and per-role-failure isolation with the sweep continuing, suffix refusal before any AWS call, fail-closed on unset stackLookupRegions). Colocated test pins manifest/version sync, the read-only single-method (audit) surface, the two specs, and the upgrade-chain invariant. 46 tests pass after the schema-strictness change.

Read-only sweep with deterministic spec-prefixed keys: role rows keyed role-<accountId>-<roleName>, scan_error rows keyed error-<profile>-<role>-<phase>, so re-runs overwrite the same instances rather than duplicating. No created cloud resources to orphan. Partial-failure safe: a per-target or per-role failure is isolated to a scan_error row and the remaining targets/roles are still audited.

ListAttachedRolePolicies and ListRolePolicies both paginated to exhaustion via IsTruncated/Marker. GetRole's NoSuchEntity is detected (by error name or message) and mapped to a null role (absence), not an error. DescribeStackResources looks up by PhysicalResourceId across the configured regions in order, taking the first owning stack; CFN's 'Stack ... does not exist' ValidationError is treated as a region miss. STS GetCallerIdentity Account defaulted to '' if absent. All AWS shapes treated as optional and defaulted before use. SDK clients configured with maxAttempts: 6 for transient-error retry.

IAM/STS/CFN clients are created within the per-account facade and used only for read calls; one CFN client per lookup region. No file handles or temp files; no created cloud resources to track. Clients are not explicitly destroy()-ed (the sibling aws-context-guard adds a try/finally destroy); minor and not a leak across the single-execution sweep, so not raised as an issue.

Re-checked after the published-surface scrub: the module JSDoc's multi-role example no longer names a real vendor — it now reads 'a compliance scanner's Readonly + ECR + EBS scanning roles' (the prior 'Aikido' reference removed). grep -niE 'sitoo|vanta|aikido|688375758630|m@s\.com' over the whole extension dir is clean. README uses explicit <account-id> / <vendor-account-id> placeholders and states no real account ids appear; example role/policy names are generic (AcmeScanner*, DemoRole). Test fixtures use synthetic 12-digit account ids (111111111111, 999999999999, 123456789012, etc.) and a generic well-known IdP label ('Okta') inside a synthetic saml-provider ARN — not a real customer identifier. No real IPs, internal hostnames, or topology in the published surface.

Now PASS after the fix. Both written resource schemas are plain z.object without .passthrough(): RoleRecordSchema (aws_iam_role_audit.ts ~line 165) and ScanErrorSchema (~line 194). Removing .passthrough() restores CEL expression validation over these CEL-queryable resources and matches the sibling aws-stackset-audit, which declares all its written schemas as plain z.object. The removal is behavior-preserving: the mandatory schema-write conformance check confirmed an exact 1:1 field match for both schemas. RoleRecordSchema has 21 fields and the row object written by writeResource('role', ...) sets exactly those 21 (the row is typed `const row: RoleRecord`, whose interface lists the same 21, so TS forbids extra keys). ScanErrorSchema has 7 fields (profile, accountId, roleName, phase, kind, message, scannedAt) and all three writeError call sites set exactly those 7 (writeError's parameter is typed ScanError with the same 7 fields). With no extra keys ever written, z.object's strip-unknown behavior is a no-op, so no data is dropped — no fields needed reconciling into the schema.
Both the role and scan_error specs declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10.

Read-only audit/discovery model, not a CRUD lifecycle. audit is a fan-out factory that re-derives role + scan_error rows each run; there is no AWS resource this model owns to create/update/delete, and re-running audit is the drift-detection mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.

audit performs only read-only calls (iam:Get*/List*, cloudformation:DescribeStackResources, sts:GetCallerIdentity). There is no mutating or remediation action a policy/live pre-flight label would gate. The requiredProfileSuffix gate (refusing profiles that do not end with e.g. -readonly, before any AWS call) is an additional read-only safety guard, not a mutation pre-flight.

Mechanical check: role keys are 'role-<accountId>-<roleName>' (one method, one shape) and scan_error keys are 'error-<profileLabel>-<roleName>-<phase>' (one writer, one shape). The two specs cannot collide (distinct 'role-' / 'error-' prefixes), and within each spec the key tuple is unique per (account, role) / (profile, role, phase).

Single-pass fan-out writer: it derives every row freshly from AWS each run and never reads back previously stored swamp state. There is no update/delete/sync path, so the readResource-vs-dataRepository concern does not arise.

Initial publish (2026.06.13.0). model.upgrades carries a single identity entry whose toVersion equals model.version; the colocated test asserts that invariant. upgradeAttributes is identity (old => old), correct for an initial publish with no prior stored instances to migrate.
